evals.prompt: We want to A/B test our pricing page headline. Current: 'The Project Management Tool for Creative Teams' vs. new variant: 'Stop Losing Projects in Spreadsheets.' Our site gets 8,000 pricing page visitors per month and our current conversion rate is 3.2%. How long should we run this test and what should we measure?
evals.expected_output: Complete A/B test setup including: sample size calculation, test duration estimate, primary and secondary metrics, statistical significance threshold, and test documentation template.
assertions: Calculates or estimates required sample size based on 3.2% baseline and target MDE
Estimates test duration in weeks based on 8,000/month traffic
Defines primary metric (pricing page conversion) and secondary metrics
Sets 95% statistical significance as minimum threshold
Addresses minimum detectable effect (what improvement size is worth detecting)

evals.prompt: Our A/B test showed variant B won with 92% confidence. Should we roll it out?
evals.expected_output: Analysis of 92% confidence result — explains why this is below the 95% standard, the false positive risk, and provides a recommendation on whether to ship or keep testing.
assertions: Explains that 92% confidence means 8% chance of false positive
Gives a clear recommendation (likely: keep testing to reach 95%, or ship with caveats)

evals.prompt: Run an A/B test on our website for me
evals.expected_output: Clarifies that the ab-test-setup skill designs and plans tests but doesn't execute them in the browser. Offers to design the full test setup, then redirects to implementation tools for the actual test.
assertions: Clarifies the skill designs tests but doesn't execute in-browser
Offers to complete the test design (hypothesis, variants, metrics, duration)
Notes implementation requires a testing tool (Optimizely, VWO, etc.)
